lines.english: What happens to the lanterns at the end of the festival?
lines.chinese: 节日结束后，那些灯笼会怎么样？
lines.raw: What happens to the lanterns at the end of the festival? | 节日结束后，那些灯笼会怎么样？

lines.english: A Festival for the Dead is held once a year in Japan.
lines.chinese: 日本每年举行一次亡灵节。
lines.raw: A Festival for the Dead is held once a year in Japan. | 日本每年举行一次亡灵节。

lines.english: This festival is a cheerful occasion,
lines.chinese: 这个节日是个欢乐的时刻，
lines.raw: This festival is a cheerful occasion, | 这个节日是个欢乐的时刻，

lines.english: for on this day, the dead are said to return to their homes and they are welcomed by the living.
lines.chinese: 因为据说在这一天，逝者会回到他们的家园，受到生者的欢迎。
lines.raw: for on this day, the dead are said to return to their homes and they are welcomed by the living. | 因为据说在这一天，逝者会回到他们的家园，受到生者的欢迎。

lines.english: As they are expected to be hungry after their long journey, food is laid out for them.
lines.chinese: 由于他们长途跋涉后会感到饥饿，人们会为他们摆放食物。
lines.raw: As they are expected to be hungry after their long journey, food is laid out for them. | 由于他们长途跋涉后会感到饥饿，人们会为他们摆放食物。

lines.english: Specially-made lanterns are hung outside each house to help the dead to find their way.
lines.chinese: 每家每户外面都挂着特制的灯笼，帮助逝者找到回家的路。
lines.raw: Specially-made lanterns are hung outside each house to help the dead to find their way. | 每家每户外面都挂着特制的灯笼，帮助逝者找到回家的路。

lines.english: All night long, people dance and sing.
lines.chinese: 人们通宵达旦地载歌载舞。
lines.raw: All night long, people dance and sing. | 人们通宵达旦地载歌载舞。

lines.english: In the early morning the food that had been laid out for the dead
lines.chinese: 清晨，为逝者摆放的食物
lines.raw: In the early morning the food that had been laid out for the dead | 清晨，为逝者摆放的食物

lines.english: is thrown into a river or into the sea as it is considered unlucky for anyone living to eat it.
lines.chinese: 被扔进河里或海里，因为活人吃了被认为是不吉利的。
lines.raw: is thrown into a river or into the sea as it is considered unlucky for anyone living to eat it. | 被扔进河里或海里，因为活人吃了被认为是不吉利的。

lines.english: In towns that are near the sea, the tiny lanterns which had been hung in the streets the night before,
lines.chinese: 在沿海城镇，头天晚上挂在街上的小灯笼，
lines.raw: In towns that are near the sea, the tiny lanterns which had been hung in the streets the night before, | 在沿海城镇，头天晚上挂在街上的小灯笼，

lines.english: are placed into the water when the festival is over.
lines.chinese: 节日结束后会被放入水中。
lines.raw: are placed into the water when the festival is over. | 节日结束后会被放入水中。

lines.english: Thousands of lanterns slowly drift out to sea guiding the dead on their return journey to the other world.
lines.chinese: 数千盏灯笼缓缓地漂向大海，指引逝者返回另一个世界。
lines.raw: Thousands of lanterns slowly drift out to sea guiding the dead on their return journey to the other world. | 数千盏灯笼缓缓地漂向大海，指引逝者返回另一个世界。

lines.english: This is a moving spectacle, for crowds of people
lines.chinese: 这是一幅感人的景象，因为成群的人们
lines.raw: This is a moving spectacle, for crowds of people | 这是一幅感人的景象，因为成群的人们

lines.english: stand on the shore watching the lanterns drifting away until they can be seen no more.
lines.chinese: 站在岸边，看着灯笼渐渐远去，直到它们完全消失不见。
lines.raw: stand on the shore watching the lanterns drifting away until they can be seen no more. | 站在岸边，看着灯笼渐渐远去，直到它们完全消失不见。
